title: Intervention selection for research instrumentation proof case
summary: The chosen intervention is a conservative pre-judgment gate at the signal-selection layer: do not emit or treat a regression candidate as skill evidence when task segmentation is missing or the outcome-definition signal is internally inconsistent.
decision_question: Which single reversible intervention should be used first for this proof case?
selection_criteria: does not mutate routing defaults or demote a skill
is already grounded in recovered evidence

candidates.name: Selection-layer gate before regression judgment
candidates.statement: Suppress regression/champion-style skill judgment when task_type is missing or outcome-definition inconsistency is detected.
weaknesses: does not by itself explain legacy partial writer

candidates.name: Redefine aggregate success semantics
candidates.statement: Change success-rate aggregation so partial outcomes no longer collapse to 0% success.
weaknesses: can break comparability with historical aggregates
still leaves missing task segmentation unresolved

candidates.name: Backfill historical task_type/session linkage
candidates.statement: Repair old rows first by reconstructing missing task_type and session_id values before judging skill quality.
weaknesses: higher epistemic risk if reconstruction guesses
slower than needed for a first proof-case intervention

candidates.name: Change runtime outcome taxonomy first
candidates.statement: Reintroduce or formalize partial at finalize time before any selection logic changes.
strengths: could eventually resolve outcome semantics at the source
weaknesses: not necessary for the first bounded intervention
historical proof gap remains even after implementation

selected_intervention.name: Segmentation and outcome-consistency gate before regression judgment
selected_intervention.statement: Before emitting or acting on a skill-level regression candidate, require non-empty task_type segmentation and block the candidate when success_rate and quality coverage indicate an outcome-definition inconsistency.
why_selected: It is the narrowest change that directly addresses the recovered evidence chain.
It reduces false-positive regression handling without changing runtime defaults.
It is easy to roll back and auditable at one clear layer.
non_goals: Do not redefine historical success semantics yet.
Do not demote the research skill.
Do not change routing defaults or champion selection globally beyond the gate.
selected_intervention.implementation_mode: selection-layer guard before regression draft emission

rollback_mechanism.strategy: Remove or revert the selection-gate invocation and restore prior draft-emission behavior.
done_when: regression draft generation no longer blocks on missing task_type
outcome-definition gate message no longer suppresses candidate creation

approval.selection_scope: Choose the proof-case intervention and record its rationale.

implementation_state.proof_case_status: selected but not logged as a dedicated proof-case change
implementation_state.note: The live codebase already contains an analogous conservative gate shape, but this proof case still lacks a dedicated change record, activation timestamp, and bounded post-change measurement.

expected_effect: null-segmented rows no longer escalate into skill-regression handling
outcome-definition mismatch is treated as instrumentation-first
Step 4 can focus on explicit change logging instead of scope debate
evidence_basis: Recovered raw rows all have task_type = null and session_id = null.
0% success is caused by counting only outcome = success while all five rows are partial.
Live gate logic already expresses the conservative decision boundary needed for this case.
follow_on_after_selection: Create one explicit proof-case change artifact with activation time and owner.
Define the bounded measurement window for post-change observations.
Document outcome semantics separately without changing aggregate history yet.
